description_long: This is a two-story apartment building with a rectangular footprint and a flat, concrete roof featuring several raised utility structures. The exterior walls are clad in red-orange brickwork, separated by a prominent white horizontal band between the floors and matching white trim at the roof and base. The front facade displays a symmetrical arrangement of six-paned windows, with three windows on each floor, and a single white door on the lower left. The rear and side facades are plain, lacking windows or doors, emphasizing the utilitarian design. The building's overall appearance is simple and functional, typical of mid-20th-century urban residential architecture.
description: A two-story red brick apartment building with a flat roof, white trim, and a symmetrical window arrangement on the front facade.
description_view_0: The side view shows a plain brick wall with no windows or doors, only the white trim bands at the base, between floors, and at the roof.
description_view_1: The front view features six windows arranged symmetrically and a single white door on the lower left, with white trim and a flat roof visible.
description_view_2: The opposite side view mirrors view 0, showing a featureless brick wall with only the white trim bands.
description_view_3: The rear view displays three windows on each floor and a single white door on the lower right, similar to the front but with the door position reversed.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are symmetrically arranged in two rows of three on the front and rear facades, evenly spaced and aligned vertically and horizontally.
